logs.summary: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has become the fastest-growing ever, with cases surging above 1,000 and the death toll reaching 600. The outbreak has spread to new provinces including Tshopo and Haut-Uele, and the first case outside Africa has been confirmed in France. Response efforts are hampered by funding shortages, unpaid health workers, and the closure of USAID, while new diagnostic tests and treatment trials offer hope.

news.headline: Médecins Sans Frontières Launches 'Keep Life Breathing' Roving Exhibition to Spotlight Humanitarian Crises and Ebola Disease Outbreak in DRC
news.body: HONG KONG, July 6, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) Hong Kong yesterday held an opening ceremony for its 'Keep Life Breathing' Roving Exhibition. As a key part of its annual 'MSF Day' campaign, the exhibition will tour three locations throughout July: The Wai, Festival Walk, and TKO Plaza. It showcases real-life stories of MSF providing emergency medical humanitarian assistance to people affected by protracted conflicts and crises. The event also features an Ebola disease-themed zone, which aims to deepen public awareness and understanding of the ongoing outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).

logs.summary:
The Ebola outbreak in the DRC has escalated, with confirmed cases surpassing 2,000 and 754 deaths, while the WHO reports the true scale may be 2 to 4 times larger. A U.S. aid worker, the second American infected in this outbreak, has tested positive for Ebola, and international health agencies are scrambling to contain the spread.

New reports indicate that the majority of Ebola cases in eastern Congo are from unknown chains of transmission, complicating containment efforts. Additionally, dozens of workers at an Ebola treatment center in northeast Congo have gone on strike over unpaid salaries, further straining the response. The case and death counts (2,011 cases, 754 deaths) remain consistent with prior reports.

logs.summary: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in eastern China’s Zhejiang province on July 12, 2026, weakening to a severe tropical storm as it moved inland. Authorities evacuated 1.7 to nearly 3 million people ahead of the storm, which brought heavy rainfall and widespread flight and railway cancellations across the Yangtze River Delta region. The storm triggered red rainstorm alerts in parts of China and continued to cause flooding and infrastructure damage.

incidents.canonical_name: Earthquake M5.9 Te Anau, New Zealand 2026-07-16
incidents.summary:
A magnitude 5.9 earthquake struck 42 km north-northwest of Te Anau, New Zealand, on July 16, 2026, initially reported as a 6.3 magnitude. The quake shook buildings across the South Island's tourist region and prompted a brief tsunami warning, which was later downgraded to a marine advisory. No injuries or damage were reported, and authorities lifted the tsunami alert shortly after.

A tsunami warning and immediate evacuation order were issued for parts of the South Island's west coast following the earthquake, but the alert has since been lifted. The mayor of the affected area reported property damage, and GeoNet recorded several lighter aftershocks after the main quake.

logs.summary:
A magnitude 5.9 earthquake struck 42 km north-northwest of Te Anau, New Zealand, on July 16, 2026, initially reported as a 6.3 magnitude. The quake shook buildings across the South Island's tourist region and prompted a brief tsunami warning, which was later downgraded to a marine advisory. No injuries or damage were reported, and authorities lifted the tsunami alert shortly after.

A tsunami warning and immediate evacuation order were issued for parts of the South Island's west coast following the earthquake, but the alert has since been lifted. The mayor of the affected area reported property damage, and GeoNet recorded several lighter aftershocks after the main quake.

incidents.summary:
A powerful magnitude 7.3–7.4 earthquake struck off the coast of Mexico's southern Chiapas state on July 17, 2026, prompting a tsunami warning for nearby coasts of Mexico and Guatemala, with waves up to one meter possible. People evacuated buildings in affected areas, while authorities confirmed no tsunami threat to Hawaii.
A powerful earthquake of magnitude 7.3–7.4 struck off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, near Puerto Madero on July 17, 2026, at a shallow depth of about 10 km. The quake triggered a tsunami warning and was strongly felt in Guatemala and El Salvador, prompting evacuations in some areas. No immediate damage or fatalities were reported, though a series of aftershocks were recorded.
New reports indicate that the 7.3-magnitude earthquake off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, caused two injuries and cracked some buildings, though no major damage or fatalities were reported.
